mapping.a_element: graph Laplacian matrix and its eigenvalues/eigenvectors
mapping.b_element: Laplacian matrix and its bottom k eigenvalues/eigenvectors

mapping.a_element: spectral clustering algorithm using eigenvectors of the Laplacian to embed vertices into R^k
mapping.b_element: embedding vertices into R^k using the bottom k eigenvectors of the Laplacian for partitioning

mapping.a_element: clustering based on geometric considerations in the embedded space
mapping.b_element: partitioning the vertex set into k subsets based on the embedded space

mapping.a_element: intuition that spectral clustering captures connected components or sparse cuts
mapping.b_element: higher-order Cheeger inequalities characterizing sparse cuts via eigenvalues close to zero

brief_justification: Both papers rely on the spectral properties of the graph Laplacian, specifically the use of its eigenvalues and eigenvectors to partition or cluster the graph. Paper A describes spectral clustering as embedding vertices into a space defined by eigenvectors, while Paper B formalizes this intuition by proving higher-order Cheeger inequalities that justify such embeddings for multi-way partitioning.
